question: How do you support data subject rights (access, rectification, erasure, portability) required by GDPR/CCPA?
title: Data Subject Rights Enablement and Operational SLAs
bullets: Describe mechanisms for each right: access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction/objection
Provide SLAs for request handling and end-to-end workflow (intake, verification, fulfillment, audit)
Explain identity verification and authorization controls for requests
Detail auditability (logs, records of processing) and customer tooling (APIs/portals)
suggested_inputs: DSAR (Data Subject Access Request) playbook and process diagrams
Audit logs or reports of DSAR handling and SLAs

good.text: We provide APIs and a customer portal for DSARs: access (export via API within defined SLA), rectification (self-service or ticketed update), erasure (verified deletion across systems within SLA), and portability (machine-readable formats like JSON/CSV). Full documentation and SLAs are published.

End-to-end DSAR workflow includes intake, identity verification, scoping across systems, execution, and confirmation. All requests are logged with immutable audit trails, timestamps, and outcomes for compliance audits.
We enforce robust identity verification (multi-factor verification, token-based validation) before fulfilling requests. Access is restricted via RBAC/ABAC and approvals for sensitive operations.

We support our customers in handling their end-users’ requests, including tooling, guidance, and operational support. We can pause processing and enforce restriction/objection requests where applicable.

partial.text: Data subject requests are handled via support tickets, with general timelines but limited automation or APIs.
We can provide data exports, but formats or schemas may not be standardized or fully machine-readable.
We keep internal records of requests, but audit trails are not comprehensive or easily accessible to customers.
We process requests, but identity verification methods and SLAs are not clearly defined or documented.

bad.text: Users should contact support for data requests without defined processes, SLAs, or tooling.
We do not interact with end-users, so data subject rights are not our responsibility.
Handling data subject rights is entirely the customer’s responsibility.
We cannot clearly explain how access, deletion, or portability requests are fulfilled.

follow_ups: Walk through an end-user access request: intake, identity verification, data discovery, fulfillment, and timeline.
Provide a sample data export (JSON/CSV/XML) and schema—confirm machine-readability.
How do you verify identity for requests and prevent unauthorized access?
If a deletion request arrives mid-processing, can you halt processing and enforce restriction immediately?
Provide audit logs/records demonstrating DSAR handling and SLA adherence.
